ops.title: yasyf-home-2 slowdown root causes (TM thinning death spiral, ~/.tart now TM-excluded, bluebubbles VM spin) and what's normal on this host
ops.body:
2026-07-18 machine-wide slowdown on yasyf-home-2, felt as slow new-binary launches. Root causes and fixes:

- **Time Machine death spiral (FIXED)**: backupd stuck in LazyThinning for 4 days at ~90% CPU because the 934GB "Local Backups" destination (91% full) could never fit the ~1.25TB source — `~/.tart` VM images (~180GB, high churn) were included in backups. Fixed: `tmutil stopbackup`, then sticky exclusions on `~/.tart` and `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.container`. If TM wedges again, check destination free space and `tmutil status` BackupPhase first.
- **Gatekeeper**: every brand-new binary costs ~200-250ms first-exec (syspolicyd assessment) on this host — normal tax, but it turns into multi-second stalls when syspolicyd is memory-starved or crashes (it segfaulted in `MachORep::signingData` on Jul 14 and Jul 18; only 2 crashes in 4 days, not chronic). Memory pressure (swap was 24.8/25.6GB full, Jetsam event 03:20) was the amplifier.
- **bluebubbles tart VM** (iMessage bridge, `com.yclaw.tart-bluebubbles` KeepAlive agent): pegged ~2 cores since Jul 14 boot — root-cause investigation was in flight at save time.

Host baseline worth knowing: a zoo of launch agents runs here — `com.yclaw.tart-{bluebubbles,hermes,metal}`, `com.yclaw.metal-nightly-bounce` (starts the 10GB "metal" VM at 05:00 daily), `com.cc-steer.{watch,pipeline,retrain}` (the MLX watcher holds ~5GB), plus OrbStack and Apple `container`. High baseline load (~5-6) is normal; don't chase it as an anomaly. Test copies of system binaries (e.g. `cp /bin/echo /tmp && exec`) die with "Launch Constraint Violation" by design — not a Gatekeeper breakage signal.
